description_long: This is a modern commercial passenger airplane with a streamlined fuselage painted primarily white, featuring the 'Hi Fly' logo on the forward section. The aircraft has two large blue turbofan engines mounted under its low, swept-back wings, which are equipped with subtle winglets at the tips. The tail section is painted blue with a pattern of white stars and consists of a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The cockpit is located at the very front of the fuselage, with a row of windows running along the sides for passengers. The landing gear is not visible in these images, suggesting it is likely retractable and currently stowed. The overall design is typical of a medium- to large-sized commercial jetliner used for urban and intercity air travel.
description: A white commercial jet airplane with blue underwing engines, swept low wings, and a blue star-patterned tail, featuring the 'Hi Fly' logo. The cockpit is forward-mounted and the design is sleek and modern.
description_view_0: Front view showing the nose, cockpit windows, and both underwing engines with the wings extending outward.
description_view_1: Left side profile displaying the full fuselage, 'Hi Fly' logo, blue engines, and the star-patterned tail fin.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the tail section, horizontal stabilizers, and the symmetry of the wings and engines.
description_view_3: Right side profile similar to the left, showing the opposite side of the fuselage, engines, and tail design.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, swept-back wings with subtle upward winglets and moderate dihedral angle.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle (not visible, inferred from typical configuration)
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multi-seat configuration and standard window arrangement
